title: Phenomenon 100
wikitext:
__NOTOC__{{VoidedTheme}} {{Background|file=Split.png}} {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=?|effectiveness=5|danger=3}}{{DISPLAYTITLE: '''Phenomenon 100''': ''"Splits"''}}
==Outerview== {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Split.png|caption=A photograph of what is thought to be a Split, taken in 1980 at [[Level 385]].}} '''Phenomenon 100''', more known as "Splits", "Ravines" or "Severance" are terms given to name a series of extremely rare, strange phenomena in unstable levels that rip them in half, eventually turning the split part into a new level. Strangely enough, '''Splits''' seem to specifically occur on the perimeter of two different regions on a level, such as in [[Level 385]]. Sometimes, a broken-apart region of a level may, instead of becoming its own unique level, it may be attracted to a neighboring level, pulling all its properties into said level and merging into it. Rarely, rather then pulled to a neighboring level, they may be attracted into a level far away. These may function as crossing points between the two levels and contain all of their properties. '''Splits''' are often described as gaping, long, darkened rips on a level, resembling a ravine throughout the level, which expands day by day. Eventually, the ravine will become exceedingly collosal, and parts of it will begin to decompose and transform into the Void; due to this, it is advised for one to never be nearby a '''Split''' ravine, due to the danger of falling. A '''Split''' will heavily modify a level's layout and properties, such as, for example, creating an isolation effect, adding a certain phenomenon, or shaking the entire area while the tissue is being ripped, separating both by [[the Void]]. While what causes this to occur is completely unknown, scientists believe that this is caused by the anomalies of other levels unknowningly traveling as undiscovered particles (that would only be reactivated when outside [[the Void]], making them pass unnoticed) through [[the Void]] and being sucked up into the '''Split''' ravine from outside. After a '''Split''', the newly formed levels will contain at least one overlapping area, a crossing point, such as the the [[Level 387|Outer Side]]. These overlapping areas are quite unstable, and remaining on them may take them to whatever other newly formed levels originated from said '''Split''', from random no-clipping to level fading. The overlapping areas seem to strangely share properties among all of the split levels, causing them to be quite hazardous and possibly unstable, other than containing unique properties, such as being anti-gravity or experiencing haphazardous fires. Strangely, after a '''Split''', the level where it occurred seems to oddly enter an era of stability, regeneration and safety for quite possibly the remaining of the level's lifespan. It is unknown why this occurs, however. ==Known Cases== As of now, there are only three known, recorded cases of '''Splits'''. (C) marks confirmed '''Splits''' and (T) are theories. ===The 380s '''Split'''; Levels [[Level 385|385]]-[[Level 387|387]]+ (C)=== The 380s '''Split''' was the first case of '''Phenomenon 100'''; it began in 1948 between [[Level 385]]'s main region and what was the "Ladder Zone," a region quite isolated from the remaining of [[Level 385]] with different properties. Initially, passing wanderers believed it to be a visual anomaly of an anomalous cloud of smoke. Eventually, the ravine became larger, and more wanderers began noticing; the outposts on the Ladder Zone were becoming quite alarmed. Researchers were sent to study the phenomenon; they eventually concluded the phenomenon occurring was a newly discovered phenomenon named "Ravine." After several decades, the phenomenon was concluded to be a level-ending occurence and that it would tear the existencial tissue of [[Level 385]], destroying it. Only in 1964-1972 was it discovered the phenomenon would not destroy the level, but something else unknown. In 1972-1974, [[Level 385]] would split in more than three, and most information detained currently would be gained from closer research. ===Several Sub-Levels (T)=== While disputed, it is believed some sub-levels (such as [[Level 483]]'s.) to have created from '''Splits''' and that this would be a variation of '''Splits''' known as "'''Double Splits''." On '''Double Splits''', two pieces of two separate levels are ripped simultaneously and collide, creating a sub-level containing features of two different levels. This is believed to have only happened twice in [[Backrooms Wiki|Backrooms]] history, and will likely never occur again. ===[[Level 128]] and [[Level 129]] (T)=== While unconfirmed, [[Level 128]] and [[Level 129]] may have been sufferers of '''Phenomenon 100'''. [[Level 128|The Correlation Theory]] and the told story of the two levels is quite similar to the 380s '''Split''' and the consequences bought by it; from the transferred properties to the sheer similarity, scientists believe the phenomenon is related to the two levels' stories deeply. The Voided Zone seems to serve and function similarly to an overlaping zone and may or may not be consumed by [[the Void]] from its appearance. The entire scenario given by tellers has been deemed similar by the researchers—even so, no photographs or reports of a '''Split''' ravine have existed on [[Level 129]]. ==Possible Next Locations== While unknown, researchers conclude the following levels, in extremely low chances, if unfortunate enough, may experience '''Splits''': *[[Level 144]]; *[[Level 866]]; *[[Level 899]]; *[[Level 483]]; *[[Level 111]]; *[[Level 386]]. ==Discovery and History== As aforementioned, '''Splits''' were discovered back in 1948 on [[Level 385]], situated on the former Ladder Zone, by passing wanderers, who eventually warned the bigger outposts, creating a domino effect. The researchers later concluded after the 380s '''Split''' that this was not the first occasion where a '''Split''' occurred, but only the first recorded one, and that more would occur. More would later be discovered with the cases of and [[Level 128]] and [[Level 129]], with strange anomalies, like overlapping zones and shared properties. Researchers would later conclude '''Splits''' occur on increasingly unstable levels and have been on the lookout exploring said levels. {{Collapse|num=100|initial=Open Author Info.|body=Author: [[User:Nightfoxy25_Animations|Nightfoxy25]]!!! *Inspired by [[Level 386]] and [[Level 385]] {{User:Nightfoxy25_Animations/Authorship}}|final=Close Author Info.}}
